claim: Use scripts in skills to handle data processing before passing to models, reducing token usage by up to 90%
actionable: Implement filtering and parsing in your skill scripts (e.g., exclude script/style/nav tags from HTML scrapes) to minimize HTML tokens sent to the model
source_title: Agent Skills: Code Beats Markdown (Here's Why)

claim: Pre-determine page structure selectors and pass them to scraping scripts rather than having the model discover them each time
actionable: Use Claude once to extract CSS selectors for your target elements, then hardcode these in your script logic for subsequent runs
source_title: Agent Skills: Code Beats Markdown (Here's Why)

claim: Return structured output (JSON/markdown) from scripts to reduce model processing overhead
actionable: Have your skill scripts output strict schema JSON (e.g., title, url, price) instead of raw data, letting the model consume formatted results
source_title: Agent Skills: Code Beats Markdown (Here's Why)

claim: Batch API calls (e.g., using threading) in scraping scripts rather than sequential requests to reduce round-trips and context passes
actionable: Use threading or asyncio in your Python scripts to make 10+ parallel API/web calls instead of sequential loops
source_title: Agent Skills: Code Beats Markdown (Here's Why)

claim: Implement pagination limits and early-stop conditions in skills to prevent context window overflow
actionable: Add max_pages or max_results parameters to your skill.md, and have scripts check conditions before looping on pagination
source_title: Agent Skills: Code Beats Markdown (Here's Why)

claim: Design scraping skills for incremental runs by tracking last-scraped state and only fetching newer data on subsequent runs
actionable: Store the most recent scraped timestamp/ID in a local file, and on next run check for updates only after that point
source_title: Agent Skills: Code Beats Markdown (Here's Why)
